page: Microsoft Azure L2aos_v4 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the Laosv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The configuration includes 1 physical core supporting 2 threads, which translates to 2 vCPUs. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB per core. The instance includes 1440 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not bundle any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data highlights a significant divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. The single-core capabilities are strong, with the stress-ng single-core CPU test scoring 3076.98 and the Geekbench single-core test scoring 2070.0, both placing in the top 25%. Conversely, multi-core and parallelized workloads show limitations; the Geekbench multi-core score of 2591.0 and the stress-ng multi-core score of 3076.98 fall into the bottom 25%. Memory write bandwidth for large blocks is average at 27.68 GB/sec, while read bandwidth is weak, measuring 151.43 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 35.96 GB/sec for large uncached blocks.

The L2aos_v4 offers a high ratio of local storage capacity relative to its 2 vCPUs, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for storage-centric workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. However, database operations perform poorly, as evidenced by a Passmark database score of 1009.2, and Redis SET operations are weak at 2.99 million operations per second. Large-scale LLM inference is also highly constrained, with medium model text generation averaging only 4.53 tokens/sec. This instance is best suited for single-threaded applications, lightweight file serving, or basic storage-heavy tasks that benefit from its high local storage capacity and strong single-core CPU performance.
description: The Microsoft Azure L2aos_v4 is a virtual machine instance from the Laosv4 family, powered by a dedicated 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture with Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization, it provides 2 vCPUs from 1 physical core, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 1440 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance, memory read bandwidth, and database operations are weak, with Passmark database operations scoring in the bottom 10%. Lacking GPU acceleration, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-heavy, single-threaded applications or lightweight file-serving tasks that leverage its high local storage capacity, rather than parallelized compute or intensive database workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L2aos_v4 is a 2-vCPU virtual machine featuring a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC processor, 16 GB RAM, and 1440 GB of local storage, offering strong single-core performance with weak multi-core capabilities.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L2aos_v4 features 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 1440 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core CPU performance for storage-heavy workloads.
tagline: An x86 virtual server combining strong single-core CPU execution with high local storage capacity for storage-focused, single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory paired with 1440 GB of local storage
Strong single-core performance placing in the top 25% of benchmarks
Weak multi-core scaling and low database operation throughput
No bundled GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses
Suited for storage-heavy, single-threaded applications and file serving
